description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the microsoft/dotnet:2.1-sdk Docker image. Define three stages: build, test, and release, and set variables BUILD_ID_CMD as `printf %05d $CI_PIPELINE_IID` and MYGET_PACKAGE_URL as https://www.myget.org/F/finitereality/api/v2/package. In the build stage add a build job that runs `dotnet build Finite.Commands.sln -c Release /p:BuildId=\"\`$BUILD_ID_CMD\`\" /p:Tag=\"$CI_COMMIT_TAG\"` then `dotnet pack Finite.Commands.sln -c Release -o ../../artifacts /p:BuildId=\"\`$BUILD_ID_CMD\`\" /p:Tag=\"$CI_COMMIT_TAG\"`, and publishes the artifacts/ directory as an artifact named $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG that expires in 1 week. In the test stage add a test job that runs `dotnet test Finite.Commands.Tests.sln -c Release /p:BuildId=\"\`$BUILD_ID_CMD\`\" /p:Tag=\"$CI_COMMIT_TAG\"`. In the release stage add a push job that depends on build, runs only on the master branch of FiniteReality/Finite.Commands, and executes a loop `for file in artifacts/*; do dotnet nuget push $file -s \"$MYGET_PACKAGE_URL\" -k \"$MYGET_API_KEY\"; done` to push packages to MyGet.
